key_risks: Semiconductor and SMH high beta (2.23) and elevated 30d volatility could amplify losses if the recent AI sentiment swings resume before exit close
Hot inflation prints (PCE 4.1%, sticky core) or hawkish July 28-29 FOMC commentary could pressure rate-sensitive cyclicals and growth
July 2 jobs report and July 14 CPI could trigger volatility against this equity-heavy allocation
Healthcare and financials recent strength may mean-revert; sector rotation could reverse the 30d leadership

portfolio.rationale: Micron's blowout Q3 (revenue $41B vs $24B prior quarter) and guidance for $50B Q4 with 86% margins, plus commentary that memory demand runs ahead of supply through 2027, support continued semiconductor strength; strong fundamental catalyst beyond price momentum.
Flash manufacturing PMI at 55.7 with output index at 59-month high, plus solid GDP revision to 2.1%, support cyclicals; positive 30d benchmark-relative return with moderate volatility.

Strongest 7d (+7.1%) and 30d (+8.0%) sector with low beta (0.38) and defensive characteristics; supported by broad sector rotation, not price alone, and offers downside protection if AI swings resume.
Solid 30d alpha (+6.3%) with low drawdown; regional banks and financials benefit from steady Fed at 3.50-3.75% and healthy claims data; lower volatility cyclical exposure.
Core benchmark exposure to capture broad market rebound after the five-day losing streak ended, controlling tracking risk against the scoring benchmark.

portfolio_rationale: Balanced tilt toward fundamentally-supported semiconductors and improving cyclicals (industrials, financials) with defensive healthcare ballast, anchored by a core S&P 500 position to manage benchmark-relative risk over the one-month window.
rationale_summary: Overweight semis on Micron's exceptional results and supply-demand backdrop, paired with cyclical and defensive sector diversification and broad market core.
